subject: Math
description: Compose a figure using simple shapes/solids and identify smaller shapes/solids within the figure.

subject: Math
description: Model shapes/solids in the world by building or drawing them.

subject: Math
description: Describe similarities and differences between two- and three-dimensional shapes/solids, in different sizes and orientations.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, compare, create, and compose shapes.

subject: Math
description: Identify shapes (squares, circles, triangles, rectangles, and hexagons) as two-dimensional and solids (cubes, cones, cylinders, and spheres) as three-dimensional.

subject: Math
description: Correctly name shapes and solids (squares, circles, triangles, rectangles, hexagons, cubes, cones, cylinders, and spheres) regardless of their orientations or overall size.

subject: Math
description: Describe objects in the environment using names of shapes and solids (squares, circles, triangles, rectangles, hexagons, cubes, cones, cylinders, and spheres). Describe the relative positions of these objects using terms such as above, below, beside, in front of, behind, between, and next to.

subject: Math
description: Identify and describe shapes and solids.

subject: Math
description: Sort a collection of objects into a given category, with 10 or fewer in each category. Compare the categories by group size.

subject: Math
description: Classify objects and count the number of objects in each category.

subject: Math
description: Identify the penny, nickel, dime, and quarter based on their attributes (size and color) and recognize the value of each.

subject: Math
description: Work with money.

subject: Math
description: Directly compare two objects with a measurable attribute in common, to describe which object has more of/less of the attribute.

subject: Math
description: Describe the measurable attributes of an object, such as length (long/short), height (tall/short), or weight (heavy/light).

subject: Math
description: Describe and compare measurable attributes.

subject: Math
description: Compose and decompose numbers from 11 to 19 into a group of ten ones and some more ones by using objects or drawings (e.g., 18 equals 10 + 8). Record the composition or decomposition using a drawing or by writing an equation.

subject: Math
description: Work with numbers 11–19 to gain foundations for place value.

subject: Math
description: Use mental strategies flexibly to develop fluency in addition and subtraction within 10.

subject: Math
description: Find the number that makes 10, when added to any given number, from 1 to 9 using objects or drawings. Record the answer using a drawing or writing an equation.

subject: Math
description: Decompose numbers less than or equal to 10 into addend pairs in more than one way (e.g., 5 = 2 + 3 and 5 = 4 + 1) by using objects or drawings. Record each decomposition using a drawing or writing an equation.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 10 to solve contextual problems with result/total unknown involving situations of add to, take from, and put together/take apart. Use objects, drawings, or equations to represent the problem.

subject: Math
description: Represent addition and subtraction with objects, fingers, drawings, acting out situations, verbal explanations, expressions, or equations.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems involving addition and subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Compare two given numbers up to 10, when written as numerals, using the terms greater than, less than, or equal to. (Students need not use comparison symbols here.)

subject: Math
description: Identify whether the number of objects in one group is greater than, less than, or equal to the number of objects in another group.

subject: Math
description: Compare numbers.

subject: Math
description: Count to answer "how many?" questions about as many as 20 things arranged in a line, a rectangular array, a circle, or as many as 10 things in a scattered configuration. Given a number from 1-20, count out that many objects.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that each successive number name refers to a quantity that is one greater and each previous number is one less.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that the last number name said tells the number of objects counted. The number of objects is the same regardless of their arrangement or the order in which they were counted.

subject: Math
description: When counting objects 1-20, say the number names in the standard order, using one-to-one correspondence.

subject: Math
description: Understand the relationship between numbers and quantities; connect counting to cardinality.

subject: Math
description: Count to tell the number of objects.

subject: Math
description: Recognize, describe, extend, and create patterns and explain a simple rule for a pattern using concrete materials. Analyze the structure of the repeating pattern by identifying the unit (core) of the pattern.

subject: Math
description: Write numbers from 0 to 20. Represent a quantity of objects with a written number 0-20.

subject: Math
description: Count forward by ones beginning from any given number within the known sequence (instead of having to begin at 1).

subject: Math
description: Count to 100 by ones, fives, and tens. Count backward from 10.

subject: Math
description: Know number names and the counting sequence.

subject: Math
description: Write mathematical arguments.

subject: Math
description: Discuss and articulate mathematical ideas.

subject: Math
description: Understand and use correct mathematical vocabulary.

subject: Math
description: Use multiple reading strategies.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two and four equal shares, describe the shares using the words halves, fourths, and quarters, and use the phrases half of, fourth of, and quarter of. Describe the whole as two of, or four of, the shares. Understand for these examples that partitioning into more equal shares creates smaller shares.

subject: Math
description: Create a composite figure and use the composite figure to make new figures by using two-dimensional shapes (rectangles, squares, hexagons, trapezoids, triangles, half-circles, and quarter-circles) or three-dimensional solids (cubes, spheres, rectangular prisms, cones, and cylinders).

subject: Math
description: Distinguish between attributes that define a shape (e.g., number of sides and vertices) versus attributes that do not define the shape (e.g., color, orientation, overall size); build and draw two-dimensional shapes to possess defining attributes.

subject: Math
description: Reason about shapes/solids and their attributes.

subject: Math
description: Organize, represent, and interpret data with up to three categories using pictographs, bar graphs, and tally charts. Ask and answer questions about the total number of data points, how many in each category, and how many more or less are in one category than in another.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Count the value of a set of like coins less than one dollar using the ¢ symbol only.

subject: Math
description: Recognize a clock as a measurement tool. Tell and write time in hours and half-hours using analog and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Work with time and money.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object using nonstandard units (paper clips, cubes, etc.) and express this length as a whole number of units.

subject: Math
description: Order three objects by length. Compare the lengths of two objects indirectly by using a third object.

subject: Math
description: Measure lengths indirectly and by iterating length units.

subject: Math
description: Subtract multiples of 10 from any number in the range of 10-99 using concrete models, drawings, strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Mentally find 10 more or 10 less than a given two-digit number without having to count by ones and explain the reasoning used.

subject: Math
description: Add a two-digit number to a one-digit number and a two-digit number to a multiple of ten (within 100). Use concrete models, drawings, strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction to explain the reasoning used.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.

subject: Math
description: Compare two two-digit numbers based on the meanings of the digits in each place and use the symbols >, =, and < to show the relationship.

subject: Math
description: Know that the digits of a two-digit number represent groups of tens and ones (e.g., 39 can be represented as 39 ones, 2 tens and 19 ones, or 3 tens and 9 ones).

subject: Math
description: Understand place value.

subject: Math
description: Recognize, describe, extend, and create patterns when counting by ones, twos, fives, and tens and use those patterns to predict the next number in the counting sequence up to 120 through counting or building with concrete materials.

subject: Math
description: Count to 120, by ones, twos, and fives starting at any multiple of that number. Count backward from 20. Read and write numbers to 120 and represent a quantity of objects with a written number.

subject: Math
description: Extend the counting sequence.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown whole number in an addition or subtraction equation with sums/differences within 20, with the unknown in any position (e.g., 8 + ? = 11, 5 = ? – 3, 6 + 6 = ?).

subject: Math
description: Understand the meaning of the equal sign (e.g., 6 = 6; 5 + 2 = 4 + 3; 7 = 8 – 1). Determine if equations involving addition and subtraction are true or false.

subject: Math
description: Work with addition and subtraction equations.

subject: Math
description: Use mental strategies flexibly and efficiently to develop fluency in addition and subtraction within 20. By the end of grade 1, know all sums and differences up to 10.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 20 using strategies such as counting on, counting back, making 10, related known facts, and composing/decomposing numbers with an emphasis on making ten (e.g., 13 – 4 = 13 – 3 – 1 = 10 – 1 = 9 or adding 6 + 7 by creating the known equivalent 6 + 4 + 3 = 10 + 3 = 13 OR 6 + 6 + 1 = 12 + 1).

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 20.

subject: Math
description: Understand the relationship between addition and subtraction by representing subtraction as an unknown-addend problem.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations (additive identity, commutative, and associative) as strategies to add and subtract. (Students need not use formal terms for these properties.)

subject: Math
description: Understand and apply properties of operations and the relationship between addition and subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Add three whole numbers whose sum is within 20 to solve contextual problems using objects, drawings, and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 20 to solve contextual problems, with unknowns in all positions, involving situations of add to, take from, put together/take apart, and compare. Use objects, drawings, and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems involving addition and subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Write mathematical arguments.

subject: Math
description: Discuss and articulate mathematical ideas.

subject: Math
description: Understand and use correct mathematical vocabulary.

subject: Math
description: Use multiple reading strategies.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two, three, and four equal shares. Describe the shares using the words halves, thirds, fourths, half of, a third of, and a fourth of, and describe the whole as two halves, three thirds, four fourths. Recognize that equal shares of identical wholes need not have the same shape.

subject: Math
description: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of same-sized squares and find the total number of squares.

subject: Math
description: Identify triangles, quadrilaterals, pentagons, and hexagons. Draw two-dimensional shapes having specified attributes (as determined directly or visually, not by measuring), such as a given number of angles/vertices or a given number of sides of equal length.

subject: Math
description: Reason about shapes and their attributes.

subject: Math
description: Draw a pictograph (with a key of values of 1, 2, 5, or 10) and a bar graph (with intervals of one) to represent a data set with up to four categories. Solve addition and subtraction problems related to the data in a graph.

subject: Math
description: Given a set of data, create a line plot, where the horizontal scale is marked off in whole-number units.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Solve contextual problems involving amounts less than one dollar including quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies using the ¢ symbol appropriately. Solve contextual problems involving whole number dollar amounts up to $100 using the $ symbol appropriately.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time in quarter hours and to the nearest five minutes (in a.m. and p.m.) using analog and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Work with time and money.

subject: Math
description: Represent whole numbers as lengths from 0 on a number line and know that the points corresponding to the numbers on the number line are equally spaced. Use a number line to represent whole number sums and differences of lengths within 100.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 100 to solve contextual problems, with the unknown in any position, involving lengths that are given in the same units by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown to represent the problem.

subject: Math
description: Relate addition and subtraction to length.

subject: Math
description: Measure, using whole number lengths, to determine how much longer one object is than another and express the difference in terms of a standard unit of length.

subject: Math
description: Estimate lengths using whole number units of inches, feet, yards, centimeters, and meters.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object using two different whole number units of measure and describe how the two measurements relate to the size of the unit chosen.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object in whole number units by selecting and using appropriate tools such as rulers, yardsticks, meter sticks, and measuring tapes.

subject: Math
description: Measure and estimate lengths in standard units.

subject: Math
description: Mentally add or subtract 10 or 100 to/from any given number within 1000.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 1000 using concrete models, drawings, strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction to explain the reasoning used. (Explanations may include words, drawing, or objects.)

subject: Math
description: Add up to four two-digit numbers using properties of operations and strategies based on place value.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 100 using properties of operations, strategies based on place value, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.

subject: Math
description: Compare two three-digit numbers based on the meanings of the digits in each place and use the symbols >, =, and < to show the relationship.

subject: Math
description: Read and write numbers to 1000 using standard form, word form, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Recognize, describe, extend, and create patterns when counting by ones, twos, fives, tens, and hundreds and use those patterns to predict the next number in the counting sequence up to 1000 through counting.

subject: Math
description: Know that the three digits of a three-digit number represent amounts of hundreds, tens, and ones (e.g., 706 can be represented in multiple ways as 7 hundreds, 0 tens, and 6 ones; 706 ones; or 70 tens and 6 ones).

subject: Math
description: Understand place value.

subject: Math
description: Identify arithmetic patterns in an addition or hundreds chart and explain them using properties of operations.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving addition and subtraction and identify and explain patterns in arithmetic.

subject: Math
description: Use repeated addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rectangular arrays with up to 5 rows and up to 5 columns; write an equation to express the total as a sum of equal addends.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether a group of objects (up to 20) has an odd or even number of members by pairing objects or counting them by 2s. Write an equation to express an even number as a sum of two equal addends.

subject: Math
description: Work with equal groups of objects to gain foundations for multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 30 using mental strategies. By the end of 2nd grade, know all sums of two one-digit numbers and related subtraction facts.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 30.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 100 to solve one- and two-step contextual problems, with unknowns in all positions, involving situations of add to, take from, put together/take apart, and compare. Use objects, drawings, and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems involving addition and subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Write mathematical arguments.

subject: Math
description: Discuss and articulate mathematical ideas.

subject: Math
description: Understand and use correct mathematical vocabulary.

subject: Math
description: Use multiple reading strategies.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.

subject: Math
description: Determine if a figure is a polygon.

subject: Math
description: Partition shapes into parts with equal areas. Recognize that equal shares of identical wholes need not have the same shape. Express the area of each part as a unit fraction of the whole.

subject: Math
description: Understand that shapes in different categories may share attributes and that the shared attributes can define a larger category. Recognize rhombuses, rectangles, and squares as examples of quadrilaterals and recognize examples of quadrilaterals that do not belong to any of these subcategories.

subject: Math
description: Reason about shapes and their attributes.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving perimeters of polygons, including finding the perimeter given the side lengths, finding an unknown side length, and exploring rectangles with the same perimeter and different areas or with the same area and different perimeters.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: recognize perimeter as an attribute of plane figures and distinguish between linear and area measures.

subject: Math
description: Recognize area as additive. Find areas of rectilinear figures by decomposing them into non-overlapping rectangles and adding the areas of the non-overlapping parts, applying this technique to solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Use tiling to show in a concrete case that the area of a rectangle with whole number side lengths a and (b + c) is the sum of (a x b) and (a x c). Use area models to represent the distributive property in mathematical reasoning.

subject: Math
description: Multiply side lengths to find areas of rectangles with whole number side lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems and represent whole-number products as rectangular areas in mathematical reasoning.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths by tiling it and show that the area is the same as would be found by multiplying the side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Relate area of rectangles to the operations of multiplication and addition.

subject: Math
description: Measure areas by counting unit squares (square centimeters, square meters, square inches, square feet, and improvised units).

subject: Math
description: Understand that a plane figure which can be covered without gaps or overlaps by n unit squares is said to have an area of n square units.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a square with side length 1 unit, called "a unit square," is said to have "one square unit" of area and can be used to measure area.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that plane figures have an area and understand concepts of area measurement.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand and apply concepts of area and relate area to multiplication and to addition.

subject: Math
description: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths using rulers marked with halves and fourths of an inch. Show the data by making a line plot, where the horizontal scale is marked off in appropriate units: whole numbers, halves, or quarters.

subject: Math
description: Draw a pictograph and a scaled bar graph to represent a data set with several categories. Solve one- and two-step "how many more" and "how many less" problems using information presented in graphs.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Measure the mass of objects and liquid volume using standard units of grams (g), kilograms (kg), milliliters (ml), and liters (l). Estimate the mass of objects and liquid volume using benchmarks.

subject: Math
description: Solve one-step contextual problems involving amounts less than one dollar including quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies using the ¢ symbol appropriately. Solve contextual problems involving whole number dollar amounts up to $1000 using the $ symbol appropriately.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the nearest minute and measure time intervals in minutes. Solve contextual problems involving addition and subtraction of time intervals in minutes.

subject: Math
description: Solve contextual problems in time and money.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving measurement and estimation of intervals of time, liquid volumes, and masses of objects.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with the same numerator or the same denominator by reasoning about their size. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Use the symbols >, =, or < to show the relationship and justify the conclusions.

subject: Math
description: Express whole numbers as fractions and recognize fractions that are equivalent to whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and generate simple equivalent fractions (e.g., ½ = 2/4, 4/6 = 2/3) and explain why the fractions are equivalent using a visual fraction model.

subject: Math
description: Understand two fractions as equivalent (equal) if they are the same size or the same point on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Explain equivalence of fractions and compare fractions by reasoning about their size.

subject: Math
description: Represent a fraction n/b on a number line diagram by marking off n lengths 1/b from 0. Recognize that the resulting interval has size n/b and that its endpoint locates the number n/b on the number line.

subject: Math
description: Represent a fraction 1/b on a number line diagram by defining the interval from 0 to 1 as the whole and partitioning it into b equal parts. Recognize that each part has size 1/b and that the endpoint locates the number 1/b on the number line.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction as a number on the number line. Represent fractions on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Understand a unit fraction, 1/b, as the quantity formed by 1 part when a whole is partitioned into b equal parts; understand a non-unit fraction, n/b, as the quantity formed by n parts of size 1/b.

subject: Math
description: Develop understanding of fractions as numbers.

subject: Math
description: Read and write multi-digit whole numbers (less than or equal to 100,000) using standard form, word form, and expanded form (e.g., 23,456 can be written as 20,000 + 3,000 + 400 + 50 + 6).

subject: Math
description: Multiply one-digit whole numbers by multiples of 10 in the range 10–90 (e.g., 9 x 80, 5 x 60) using strategies based on place value and properties of operations.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 1000 using strategies and algorithms based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Round whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100 using understanding of place value and use a number line to explain how the number was rounded.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic.

subject: Math
description: Identify patterns in a multiplication chart and explain them using properties of operations.

subject: Math
description: Solve two-step contextual problems using the four operations. Represent these problems using equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity. Assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computation and estimation strategies including rounding.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving the four operations and identify and explain patterns in arithmetic.

subject: Math
description: Fluently multiply and divide within 100, using strategies such as the properties of operations or the relationship between multiplication and division (e.g., knowing that 8 x 5 = 40, one knows 40 ÷ 5 = 8). By the end of 3rd grade, know all products of two one-digit numbers and related division facts.

subject: Math
description: Multiply and divide within 100.

subject: Math
description: Understand division as an unknown-factor problem.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to multiply and divide. (Students need not use formal terms for these properties.)

subject: Math
description: Understand properties of multiplication and the relationship between multiplication and division.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown whole number in a multiplication or division equation relating three whole numbers within 100.

subject: Math
description: Multiply and divide within 100 to solve contextual problems, with the unknown in any positions, in situations involving equal groups, arrays/area, and measurement quantities using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and the relationship between multiplication and division (e.g., contexts including computations such as 3 x ? = 24, 6 x 16 = ?, ? ÷ 8 = 3, or 96 ÷ 6 = ?).

subject: Math
description: Interpret the dividend, divisor, and quotient in whole number division equations (e.g., 28 ÷ 7 can be interpreted as 28 objects divided into 7 equal groups with 4 objects in each group or 28 objects divided so there are 7 objects in each of the 4 equal groups).

subject: Math
description: Interpret the factors and products in whole number multiplication equations (e.g., 4 x 7 is 4 groups of 7 objects with a total of 28 objects or 4 strings measuring 7 inches each with a total length of 28 inches).

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems involving multiplication and division.

subject: Math
description: Write mathematical arguments.

subject: Math
description: Discuss and articulate mathematical ideas.

subject: Math
description: Understand and use correct mathematical vocabulary.

subject: Math
description: Use multiple reading strategies.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and draw lines of symmetry for two-dimensional figures.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures based on the presence or absence of parallel or perpendicular lines or the presence or absence of angles of a specified size. Classify triangles based on the measure of the angles as right, acute, or obtuse.

subject: Math
description: Draw points, lines, line segments, rays, angles (right, acute, obtuse, straight, reflex), and perpendicular and parallel lines. Identify these in two-dimensional figures.

subject: Math
description: Draw and identify lines and angles and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angle measure as additive. When an angle is decomposed into non-overlapping parts, the angle measure of the whole is the sum of the angle measures of the parts. Solve addition and subtraction problems to find unknown angles on a diagram in real-world and mathematical problems. (e.g., by using an equation with a symbol for the unknown angle measure).

subject: Math
description: Measure angles in whole-number degrees using a protractor. Sketch angles of specified measure.

subject: Math
description: Understand that an angle that turns through 1/360 of a circle is called a "one degree angle," and can be used to measure angles. An angle that turns through n one-degree angles is said to have an angle measure of n degrees and represents a fractional portion of the circle.

subject: Math
description: Understand that an angle is measured with reference to a circle with its center at the common endpoint of the rays, by considering the fraction of the circular arc between the points where the two rays intersect the circle.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angles as geometric shapes that are formed wherever two rays share a common endpoint; and understand concepts of angle measurement.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of angle and measure angles.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of the same unit (½ or ¼ or 1/8). Use operations on fractions for this grade to solve problems involving information presented in line plots.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Know and apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles in real world and mathematical contexts.

subject: Math
description: Solve one- or two-step real-world problems involving whole number measurements (including length, liquid volume, mass/weight, time, and money) with all four operations within a single system of measurement. (Contexts need not include conversions.)

subject: Math
description: Measure and estimate to determine relative sizes of measurement units within a single system of measurement involving length, liquid volume, and mass/weight of objects using customary and metric units.

subject: Math
description: Estimate and solve problems involving measurement.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to hundredths by reasoning about their size. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two decimals refer to the same whole. Use the symbols >, =, or < to show the relationship and justify the conclusions.

subject: Math
description: Read and write decimal notation for fractions with denominators 10 or 100. Locate these decimals on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Express a fraction with denominator 10 as an equivalent fraction with denominator 100, and use this technique to add two fractions with respective denominators 10 and 100.

subject: Math
description: Understand decimal notation for fractions and compare decimal fractions.

subject: Math
description: Solve contextual problems involving multiplication of a whole number by a fraction (e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem).

subject: Math
description: Understand a multiple of a/b as a multiple of 1/b and use this understanding to multiply a whole number by a fraction.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction a/b as a multiple of 1/b.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend understanding of multiplication as repeated addition to multiply a whole number by a fraction.

subject: Math
description: Solve contextual problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole and having like denominators

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract mixed numbers with like denominators by replacing each mixed number with an equivalent fraction and/or by using properties of operations and the relationship between addition and subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Decompose a fraction into a sum of fractions with the same denominator in more than one way (e.g., 3/8 = 1/8 + 1/8 + 1/8; 3/8 = 1/8 + 2/8; 2 1/8 = 1 + 1 + 1/8 = 8/8 + 8/8 + 1/8) recording each decomposition by an equation. Justify decompositions using a visual fraction model.

subject: Math
description: Understand addition and subtraction of fractions as joining and separating parts referring to the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction a/b with a > 1 as a sum of fractions 1/b.

subject: Math
description: Build fractions from unit fractions by applying and extending previous understandings of operations on whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators by creating common denominators or common numerators or by comparing to a benchmark such as 0 or ½ or 1. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Use the symbols >, =, or < to show the relationship and justify the conclusions.

subject: Math
description: Explain why a fraction a/b is equivalent to a fraction (a x n)/(b x n) or (a ÷ n)/(b ÷ n) using visual fraction models, with attention to how the number and size of the parts differ even though the two fractions themselves are the same size. Use this principle to recognize and generate equivalent fractions.

subject: Math
description: Extend understanding of fraction equivalence and comparison.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients and remainders with up to four-digit dividends and one-digit divisors, using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.

subject: Math
description: Multiply a whole number of up to four digits by a one-digit whole number and multiply two two-digit numbers, using strategies based on place value and the properties of operations. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 1,000,000 using efficient strategies and algorithms.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic.

subject: Math
description: Round multi-digit whole numbers to any place (up to and including the hundred-thousand place) using understanding of place value and use a number line to explain how the number was rounded.

subject: Math
description: Read and write multi-digit whole numbers (less than or equal to 1,000,000) using standard form, word form, and expanded notation (e.g. the expanded notation of 4256 is written as (4 x 1000) + (2 x 100) + (5 x 10) + (6 x 1)). Compare two multi-digit numbers based on meanings of the digits in each place and use the symbols >, =, and < to show the relationship.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that in a multi-digit whole number (less than or equal to 1,000,000), a digit in one place represents 10 times as much as it represents in the place to its right.

subject: Math
description: Generalize place value understanding for multidigit whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Generate a number or shape pattern that follows a given rule. Identify apparent features of the pattern that were not explicit in the rule itself.

subject: Math
description: Generate and analyze patterns.

subject: Math
description: Find factor pairs for whole numbers in the range 1–100 using models. Recognize that a whole number is a multiple of each of its factors. Determine whether a given whole number is prime or composite and whether the given number is a multiple of a given one-digit number.

subject: Math
description: Gain familiarity with factors and multiples.

subject: Math
description: Solve multi-step contextual problems (posed with whole numbers and having whole-number answers using the four operations) including problems in which remainders must be interpreted. Represent these problems using equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity.

subject: Math
description: Multiply or divide to solve contextual problems involving multiplicative comparison, and distinguish multiplicative comparison from additive comparison

subject: Math
description: Interpret a multiplication equation as a comparison (e.g., interpret 35 = 5 x 7 as a statement that 35 is 5 times as many as 7 and 7 times as much as 5). Represent verbal/written statements of multiplicative comparisons as multiplication equations.

subject: Math
description: Use the four operations with whole numbers to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Write mathematical arguments.

subject: Math
description: Discuss and articulate mathematical ideas.

subject: Math
description: Understand and use correct mathematical vocabulary.

subject: Math
description: Use multiple reading strategies.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy based on properties. Understand that attributes belonging to a category of two-dimensional figures also belong to all subcategories of that category.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures into categories based on their properties.

subject: Math
description: Represent real-world and mathematical problems by graphing points in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane and interpret coordinate values of points in the context of the situation.

subject: Math
description: Graph ordered pairs and label points using the first quadrant of the coordinate plane. Understand in the ordered pair that the first number indicates the horizontal distance traveled along the x-axis from the origin and the second number indicates the vertical distance traveled along the y-axis, with the convention that the names of the two axes and the coordinates correspond (e.g., x-axis and x-coordinate, y-axis and y-coordinate).

subject: Math
description: Graph points on the coordinate plane to solve real-world and mathematical problems.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as additive. Find volumes of solid figures composed of two non-overlapping right rectangular prisms by adding the volumes of the nonoverlapping parts, applying this technique to solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Know and apply the formulas V = l x w x h and V = B x h (where B represents the area of the base) for rectangular prisms with whole number edge lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with whole-number side lengths by packing it with unit cubes and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths, equivalently by multiplying the height by the area of the base. Represent whole-number products of three factors as volumes (e.g., to represent the associative property of multiplication).

subject: Math
description: Relate volume to the operations of multiplication and addition and solve real-world and mathematical problems involving volume of right rectangular prisms.

subject: Math
description: Measure volume by counting unit cubes, using cubic centimeters, cubic inches, cubic feet, and improvised units.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a solid figure which can be packed without gaps or overlaps using n unit cubes is said to have a volume of n cubic units.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a cube with side length 1 unit, called a "unit cube," is said to have "one cubic unit" of volume and can be used to measure volume.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as an attribute of solid figures and understand concepts of volume measurement.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of volume and relate volume to multiplication and to addition.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (½, ¼, 1/8). Use operations on fractions for this grade to solve problems involving information presented in line plots.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Convert customary and metric measurement units within a single system by expressing measurements of a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit. Use these conversions to solve multi-step real-world problems involving distances, intervals of time, liquid volumes, masses of objects, and money (including problems involving simple fractions or decimals).

subject: Math
description: Convert like measurement units within a given measurement system from a larger unit to a smaller unit.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving division of unit fractions by non-zero whole numbers and division of whole numbers by unit fractions and non-unit fractions by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a whole number by a unit fraction and compute such quotients.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a unit fraction by a non-zero whole number and compute such quotients.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of division to divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving multiplication of fractions and mixed numbers by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.

subject: Math
description: Explain why multiplying a given number by a fraction greater than 1 results in a product greater than the given number (recognizing multiplication by whole numbers greater than 1 as a familiar case); explain why multiplying a given number by a fraction between 0 and 1 results in a product less than the given number; and relate the principle of fraction equivalence a/b = (a x n)/(b x n) to the effect of multiplying a/b by 1.

subject: Math
description: Compare the size of a product to the size of one factor on the basis of the size of the other factor, without performing the indicated multiplication. For example, know if the product will be greater than, less than, or equal to the factors.

subject: Math
description: Interpret multiplication as scaling (resizing).

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with fractional side lengths by tiling it with unit squares of the appropriate unit fraction side lengths, and show that the area is the same as would be found by multiplying the side lengths. Multiply fractional side lengths to find areas of rectangles and represent fraction products as rectangular areas.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the product a/b x q as a x (q ÷ b) (partition the quantity q into b equal parts and then multiply by a). Interpret the product a/b x q as (a x q) ÷ b (multiply a times the quantity q and then partition the product into b equal parts).

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication to multiply a fraction by a whole number or a fraction by a fraction.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a fraction as division of the numerator by the denominator (a/b = a ÷ b).

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to multiply and divide fractions.

subject: Math
description: Solve contextual problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole, including cases of unlike denominators. Use benchmark fractions and number sense of fractions to estimate mentally and assess the reasonableness of answers.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators (including mixed numbers) by replacing given fractions with equivalent fractions in such a way as to produce an equivalent sum or difference of fractions with like denominators.

subject: Math
description: Use equivalent fractions as a strategy to add and subtract fractions.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between operations. Assess the reasonableness of answers using estimation strategies. (Limit multiplication problems so that the product does not exceed thousandths. Limit division problems so that either the dividend or the divisor is a whole number.)

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients and remainders of whole numbers with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors, using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.

subject: Math
description: Fluently multiply multi-digit whole numbers (up to three-digit by four-digit factors) using efficient strategies and algorithms.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations with multi-digit whole numbers and with decimals to hundredths.

subject: Math
description: Round decimals to the nearest hundredth, tenth, or whole number using understanding of place value, and use a number line to explain how the number was rounded.

subject: Math
description: Read and write decimals to thousandths using standard form, word form, and expanded notation (e.g., the expanded notation of 347.392 is written as (3 x 100) + (4 x 10) + (7 x 1) + (3 x (1/10)) + (9 x (1/100)) + (2 x (1/1000))). Compare two decimals to thousandths based on meanings of the digits in each place and use the symbols >, =, and < to show the relationship.

subject: Math
description: Explain patterns in the number of zeros of the product when multiplying a number by powers of 10, and explain patterns in the placement of the decimal point when a decimal is multiplied or divided by a power of 10. Use whole-number exponents to denote powers of 10.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that in a multi-digit number, a digit in one place represents 10 times as much as it represents in the place to its right and 1/10 of what it represents in the place to its left.

subject: Math
description: Understand the place value system.

subject: Math
description: Form ordered pairs (limited to first quadrant) consisting of corresponding terms from two numerical patterns and graph the ordered pairs on a coordinate plane.

subject: Math
description: Identify relationships between corresponding terms in two numerical patterns.

subject: Math
description: Generate two numerical patterns using two given rules.

subject: Math
description: Analyze patterns and relationships.

subject: Math
description: Write numerical expressions that record calculations with numbers and interpret numerical expressions without evaluating them.

subject: Math
description: Use parentheses and/or brackets in numerical expressions involving whole numbers and evaluate expressions having these symbols using the conventional order by applying the Order of Operations. (When applying the order of operations, the evaluation of exponents need not be included.)

subject: Math
description: Write and interpret numerical expressions.

subject: Math
description: Relate the choice of measures of center to the shape of the data distribution and the context in which the data were gathered.

subject: Math
description: Give quantitative measures of center (median and/or mean) and variability (range) as well as describing any overall pattern with reference to the context in which the data were gathered.

subject: Math
description: Describe the nature of the attribute under investigation, including how it was measured and its units of measurement.

subject: Math
description: Report the number of observations.

subject: Math
description: Summarize numerical data sets in relation to their context.

subject: Math
description: Display a single set of numerical data using dot plots (line plots), box plots, pie charts and stem plots.

subject: Math
description: Summarize and describe distributions.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that a measure of center (mean, median, mode) for a numerical data set summarizes all of its values with a single number, while a measure of variation describes how its values vary with a single number.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a set of data collected to answer a statistical question has a distribution which can be described by its measures of center (mean, median, mode), measures of variation (range only), and overall shape.

subject: Math
description: Recognize a statistical question as one that anticipates variability in the data related to the question and accounts for it in the answers.

subject: Math
description: Develop understanding of statistical variability.

subject: Math
description: Represent three-dimensional figures using nets made up of rectangles and triangles, and use the nets to find the surface area of these figures. Apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.

subject: Math
description: Draw polygons in the coordinate plane given coordinates for the vertices; use coordinates to find the length of a side that joins two vertices (vertical or horizontal segments only). Apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with fractional edge lengths by packing it with unit cubes of the appropriate unit fraction edge lengths, and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths of the prism. Apply the formulas V = lwh and V = Bh where B is the area of the base to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with fractional edge lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of right triangles, other triangles, special quadrilaterals, and polygons by composing into rectangles or decomposing into triangles and other shapes; know and apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving area, surface area, and volume.

subject: Math
description: Analyze the relationship between the dependent and independent variables using graphs and tables, and relate these to the equation.

subject: Math
description: Write an equation in the form of y = px where y, p, and x are all non-negative and p ≠ 0, to express one quantity, thought of as the dependent variable, in terms of the other quantity, thought of as the independent variable.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent two quantities in a real-world problem that change in relationship to one another.

subject: Math
description: Represent and analyze quantitative relationships between dependent and independent variables.

subject: Math
description: Interpret and write an inequality of the form x > c, x < c, x ≤ c, or x ≥ c which represents a condition or constraint in a real-world or mathematical problem. Recognize that inequalities have infinitely many solutions; represent solutions of inequalities on number line diagrams.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems by writing and solving one-step equations of the form x + p = q, px = q, x – p = q, and x/p = q for cases in which p, q, and x are all non-negative rational numbers and p ≠ 0. (Complex fractions are not an expectation at this grade level.)

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent numbers and write expressions when solving real-world and mathematical problems; understand that a variable can represent an unknown number, or, depending on the purpose at hand, any number in a specified set.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a solution to an equation or inequality is the value(s) that makes that statement true. Use substitution to determine whether a given number in a specified set makes an equation or inequality true.

subject: Math
description: Reason about and solve one-variable equations and inequalities.

subject: Math
description: Identify when expressions are equivalent (i.e., when the expressions name the same number regardless of which value is substituted into them).

subject: Math
description: Apply the properties of operations (including, but not limited to, commutative, associative, and distributive properties) to generate equivalent expressions. (The distributive property of multiplication over addition is prominent here. Negative coefficients are not an expectation at this grade level.)

subject: Math
description: Evaluate expressions at specific values of their variables. Include expressions that arise from formulas used in real-world problems. Perform arithmetic operations, including those involving whole number exponents, in the conventional order when there are no parentheses to specify a particular order (Order of Operations).

subject: Math
description: Identify parts of an expression using mathematical terms (sum, term, product, factor, quotient, coefficient); view one or more parts of an expression as a single entity.

subject: Math
description: Write expressions that record operations with numbers and with variables.

subject: Math
description: Write, read, and evaluate expressions in which variables stand for numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write and evaluate numerical expressions involving whole-number exponents.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of arithmetic to algebraic expressions.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems by graphing points in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane. Include use of coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate.

subject: Math
description: Understand the absolute value of a rational number as its distance from 0 on the number line and distinguish comparisons of absolute value from statements about order in a real-world context.

subject: Math
description: Write, interpret, and explain statements of order for rational numbers in real world contexts.

subject: Math
description: Interpret statements of inequality as statements about the relative position of two numbers on a number line diagram.

subject: Math
description: Understand ordering and absolute value of rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Find and position integers and other rational numbers on a horizontal or vertical number line diagram; find and position pairs of integers and other rational numbers on a coordinate plane.

subject: Math
description: Understand signs of numbers in ordered pairs as indicating locations in quadrants of the coordinate plane; recognize that when two ordered pairs differ only by signs, the locations of the points are related by reflections across one or both axes.

subject: Math
description: Recognize opposite signs of numbers as indicating locations on opposite sides of 0 on the number line; recognize that the opposite of the opposite of a number is the number itself.

subject: Math
description: Understand a rational number as a point on the number line. Extend number line diagrams and coordinate axes familiar from previous grades to represent points on the line and in the plane with negative number coordinates.

subject: Math
description: Understand that positive and negative numbers are used together to describe quantities having opposite directions or values (e.g., temperature above/below zero, elevation above/below sea level, credits/debits, positive/negative electric charge); use positive and negative numbers to represent quantities in real world contexts, explaining the meaning of 0 in each situation as well as describing situations in which opposite quantities can combine to make 0.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of numbers to the system of rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Find the greatest common factor of two whole numbers less than or equal to 100 and the least common multiple of two whole numbers less than or equal to 12. Use the distributive property to express a sum of two whole numbers 1–100 with a common factor as a multiple of a sum of two whole numbers with no common factor.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add, subtract, multiply, and divide multi-digit decimals using a standard algorithm and making connections to previous conceptual work with each operation.

subject: Math
description: Fluently divide multi-digit numbers using a standard algorithm.

subject: Math
description: Compute fluently with multi-digit numbers and find common factors and multiples.

subject: Math
description: Interpret and compute quotients of fractions, and solve real-world and mathematical problems involving division of fractions by fractions (e.g., connecting visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem is suggested).

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to divide fractions by fractions.

subject: Math
description: Use ratio reasoning to convert customary and metric measurement units (within the same system); manipulate and transform units appropriately when multiplying or dividing quantities.

subject: Math
description: Find a percent of a quantity as a rate per 100 (e.g., 30% of a quantity means 30/100 times the quantity); solve problems involving finding the whole, given a part and the percent.

subject: Math
description: Solve unit rate problems including those involving unit pricing and constant speed.

subject: Math
description: Make tables of equivalent ratios relating quantities with whole number measurements, find missing values in the tables, and plot the pairs of values on the coordinate plane. Use tables to compare ratios.

subject: Math
description: Use ratio and rate reasoning to solve real-world and mathematical problems (e.g., by reasoning about tables of equivalent ratios, tape diagrams, double number line diagrams, or equations).

subject: Math
description: Understand the concept of a unit rate a/b associated with a ratio a:b with b ≠ 0. Use rate language in the context of a ratio relationship.

subject: Math
description: Understand the concept of a ratio and use ratio language to describe a ratio relationship between two quantities. Make a distinction between ratios and fractions.

subject: Math
description: Understand ratio concepts and use ratio reasoning to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Relate and understand the choice of measures of center (median and/or mean) and variability (range and/or interquartile range) to the shape of the data distribution and the context in which the data were gathered.

subject: Math
description: Give quantitative measures of center (median and/or mean) and variability (range and/or interquartile range), as well as describe any overall pattern and any striking deviations from the overall pattern with reference to the context in which the data were gathered.

subject: Math
description: Summarize a numerical data set in relation to its context.

subject: Math
description: Summarize and describe numerical data sets.

subject: Math
description: Develop a probability model, including non-uniform models, by observing frequencies in data generated from a chance process. Use the model to estimate the probabilities of events.

subject: Math
description: Use a uniform probability model, with equal probability assigned to all outcomes, to determine probabilities of events.

subject: Math
description: Develop a probability model and use it to find experimental or theoretical probabilities of events.

subject: Math
description: Compare theoretical probabilities to experimental probabilities; explain any possible sources of discrepancy.

subject: Math
description: Calculate the theoretical probability of a simple event.

subject: Math
description: Approximate the probability of a chance event by collecting data on the chance process that produces it and observing its long-run relative frequency, and predict the approximate relative frequency given the probability.

subject: Math
description: Calculate theoretical and experimental probability of simple events.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that the probability of a chance event is a number between 0 and 1 and interpret the likelihood of the event occurring.

subject: Math
description: Investigate chance processes and develop, use, and evaluate probability models.

subject: Math
description: Use measures of center and measures of variability for numerical data from random samples to draw informal comparative inferences about two populations.

subject: Math
description: Informally compare the measures of center (mean, median, mode) of two numerical data distributions with similar variabilities.

subject: Math
description: Draw informal comparative inferences about two populations.

subject: Math
description: Collect and use data from a random sample to draw inferences about a population with an unknown characteristic of interest. Generate multiple samples (or simulated samples) of the same size to gauge the variation in estimates or predictions.

subject: Math
description: Explore how statistics can be used to gain information about a population by examining a sample of the population; generalizations about a population from a sample are valid only if the sample is representative of that population. Understand that random sampling tends to produce representative samples and support valid inferences.

subject: Math
description: Use random sampling to draw inferences about a population.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving area of two-dimensional figures composed of triangles, quadrilaterals, and polygons, and volume and surface area of three-dimensional objects composed of cubes and right prisms.

subject: Math
description: Know and use facts about supplementary, complementary, vertical, and adjacent angles in a multi-step problem to write and solve simple equations for an unknown angle in a figure.

subject: Math
description: Know the formulas for the area and circumference of a circle and use them to solve problems. Explore the relationships between the radius, the circumference, and the area of a circle, and the number π.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving angle measure, area, surface area, and volume.

subject: Math
description: Draw triangles with given conditions: three angle measures or three side measures. Notice when the conditions determine a unique triangle, more than one triangle, or no triangle.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving scale drawings of congruent and similar geometric figures, including computing actual lengths and areas from a scale drawing and reproducing a scale drawing at a different scale.

subject: Math
description: Draw, construct, and describe geometrical figures and describe the relationships between them.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems leading to inequalities of the form px + q > r, px + q < r, px + q ≥ r, and px + q ≤ r, where p, q, and r are specific rational numbers. Graph the solution set of the inequality on a number line and interpret it in the context of the problem.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems leading to equations of the form px + q = r and p(x + q) = r where p, q, and r are specific rational numbers. Solve equations of these forms fluently. Compare an algebraic solution to an arithmetic solution, identifying the sequence of the operations used in each approach.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent quantities in a real-world and mathematical problem, and construct simple equations and inequalities to solve problems by reasoning about the quantities.

subject: Math
description: Assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computation and estimation strategies.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations to calculate with numbers in any form; convert between forms as appropriate.

subject: Math
description: Solve multi-step real-world and mathematical problems posed with positive and negative rational numbers presented in any form (whole numbers, fractions, and decimals).

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems using numerical and algebraic expressions and equations and inequalities.

subject: Math
description: Rewrite and connect equivalent expressions in different forms in a contextual problem to provide multiple ways of interpreting the problem and investigating how the quantities in it are related.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to add, subtract, factor, and expand linear expressions with rational coefficients.

subject: Math
description: Use properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving the four operations with rational numbers. (Computations with rational numbers extend the rules for manipulating fractions to complex fractions.)

subject: Math
description: Convert a rational number to a decimal using long division; know that the decimal form of a rational number terminates or eventually repeats.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to multiply and divide rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand that integers can be divided, provided that the divisor is not zero, and every quotient of integers (with non-zero divisor) is a rational number. If p and q are integers, then –(p/q) = (–p)/q = p/(–q). Interpret quotients of rational numbers by describing real-world contexts.

subject: Math
description: Understand that multiplication is extended from fractions to all rational numbers by requiring that operations continue to satisfy the properties of operations, particularly the distributive property, leading to products such as (– 1)(–1) = 1 and the rules for multiplying signed numbers. Interpret products of rational numbers by describing real-world contexts.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division and of fractions to multiply and divide rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to add and subtract rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand subtraction of rational numbers as adding the additive inverse, p – q = p + (–q). Show that the distance between two rational numbers on the number line is the absolute value of their difference, and apply this principle in real-world contexts.

subject: Math
description: Understand p + q as the number located a distance |q| from p, in the positive or negative direction depending on whether q is positive or negative. Show that a number and its opposite have a sum of 0 (are additive inverses). Interpret sums of rational numbers by describing real- world contexts.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of addition and subtraction to add and subtract rational numbers; represent addition and subtraction on a horizontal or vertical number line diagram.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of operations with fractions to add, subtract, multiply, and divide rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use proportional relationships to solve multi-step ratio and percent problems.

subject: Math
description: Explain what a point (x, y) on the graph of a proportional relationship means in terms of the situation, with special attention to the points (0, 0) and (1, r) where r is the unit rate.

subject: Math
description: Use the concept of equality to represent proportional relationships with equations.

subject: Math
description: Identify the constant of proportionality (unit rate) in tables, graphs, equations, diagrams, and verbal descriptions of proportional relationships.

subject: Math
description: Decide whether two quantities are in a proportional relationship (e.g., by testing for equivalent ratios in a table or graphing on a coordinate plane and observing whether the graph is a straight line through the origin).

subject: Math
description: Recognize and represent proportional relationships between quantities.

subject: Math
description: Compute unit rates associated with ratios of fractions, including ratios of lengths, areas, and other quantities measured in like or different units.

subject: Math
description: Analyze proportional relationships and use them to solve real-world and mathematical problems.

subject: Math
description: Represent sample spaces for compound events using methods such as organized lists, tables, and tree diagrams. For an event described in everyday language (e.g., "rolling double sixes"), identify the outcomes in the sample space which compose the event.

subject: Math
description: Understand that, just as with simple events, the probability of a compound event is the fraction of outcomes in the sample space for which the compound event occurs.

subject: Math
description: Find probabilities of and represent sample spaces for compound events using organized lists, tables, tree diagrams, and simulation.

subject: Math
description: Investigate chance processes and develop, use, and evaluate probability models

subject: Math
description: Use the equation of a linear model to solve problems in the context of bivariate measurement data, interpreting the slope and intercepts.

subject: Math
description: Know that straight lines are widely used to model linear relationships between two quantitative variables. For scatter plots that suggest a linear association, informally fit a straight line and informally assess the model fit by judging the closeness of the data points to the line.

subject: Math
description: Construct and interpret scatter plots for bivariate measurement data to investigate patterns of association between two quantities. Describe patterns such as clustering, outliers, positive or negative association, linear association, and nonlinear association.

subject: Math
description: Investigate patterns of association in bivariate data.

subject: Math
description: Apply the formulas for the volumes of cones, cylinders, and spheres to solve real-world and mathematical problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving volume of cylinders, cones, and spheres.

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Explain a model of the Pythagorean Theorem and its converse.

subject: Math
description: Understand and apply the Pythagorean Theorem.

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Make connections between dilations and scale factors.

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Verify informally that angles are taken to angles of the same measure.

description: Verify informally that lines are taken to lines, and determine when line segments are taken to line segments of the same length.

subject: Math
description: Understand and describe the effects of transformations on two-dimensional figures and use informal arguments to establish facts about angles.

subject: Math
description: Describe qualitatively the functional relationship between two quantities by analyzing a graph (e.g., where the function is increasing or decreasing, linear or nonlinear). Sketch a graph that exhibits the qualitative features of a function that has been described verbally.

subject: Math
description: Construct a function to model a linear relationship between two quantities. description: Use functions to model relationships between quantities.
